title: Special Combat Situations
description: Rules for mounted combat, underwater fighting, and other special scenarios

mounted_combat.description: A willing creature that is at least one size larger than a rider and that has an appropriate anatomy can serve as a mount

mounting_dismounting.description: During your move, you can mount a creature that is within 5 feet of you or dismount
mounting_dismounting.cost: Doing so costs an amount of movement equal to half your Speed (round down)
mounting_dismounting.example: For example, if your Speed is 30 feet, you spend 15 feet of movement to mount a horse

controlling_mount.training_requirement: You can control a mount only if it has been trained to accept a rider. Domesticated horses, mules, and similar creatures have such training
controlling_mount.initiative_change: The Initiative of a controlled mount changes to match yours when you mount it
controlling_mount.movement_and_actions: It moves on your turn as you direct it, and it has only three action options during that turn: Dash, Disengage, and Dodge
controlling_mount.immediate_action: A controlled mount can move and act even on the turn that you mount it

independent_mount.description: In contrast, an independent mount—one that lets you ride but ignores your control—retains its place in the Initiative order and moves and acts as it likes

falling_off.forced_movement: If an effect is about to move your mount against its will while you're on it, you must succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity saving throw or fall off, landing with the Prone condition in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the mount
falling_off.prone_condition: While mounted, you must make the same save if you're knocked Prone or the mount is

underwater_combat.description: A fight underwater follows these rules

impeded_weapons.melee_attacks: When making a melee attack roll with a weapon underwater, a creature that lacks a Swim Speed has Disadvantage on the attack roll unless the weapon deals Piercing damage
impeded_weapons.ranged_attacks: A ranged attack roll with a weapon underwater automatically misses a target beyond the weapon's normal range, and the attack roll has Disadvantage against a target within normal range

underwater_combat.fire_resistance: Anything underwater has Resistance to Fire damage

considerations: Fall damage when flight is disrupted
Line of sight and cover from aerial positions
Risk of falling when taking damage

effects: Attacks against prone creatures have advantage if within 5 feet
Ranged attacks against prone creatures have disadvantage
Movement costs extra to stand up
Blinded condition for creatures without darkvision
Disadvantage on attack rolls against unseen targets
Advantage on attack rolls when unseen by target

large_vs_small.description: Combat between creatures of very different sizes

considerations: Vehicle AC, HP, and damage threshold
Area effect spell targeting multiple enemies
